prevalence.trend: stable; higher in women and older adults

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria; exclude secondary causes and alarm features

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with diet and laxative regimens

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care diagnosis with GI referral as needed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary fiber and fluids, osmotic laxatives, stimulant laxatives short-term
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Adherence and long-term management in older adults
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide access to effective laxatives and primary care
